hero.title: Start your Next.js project with language and theme preferences in place.
hero.description: NextZero is a ready-to-run Next.js template that demonstrates how to integrate internationalization, theme presets, and appearance preferences. Choose and extend the languages and design tokens that fit your product.

demo.title: See language, theme, and mode in one interface
demo.languages: Configure and extend for your product

themeGallery.title: Browse ready-to-use styles as they flow by.
themeGallery.description: Hover to pause the gallery, then click any theme to apply it instantly.

features.title: Set up preferences once, then focus on the product.
features.description: Keep language selection, theme tokens, and color modes in one configuration from the first day of the project.

items.title: Internationalization
items.description: Language configuration, copy resources, browser-language detection, and an in-app picker are ready to adapt to your product.

items.title: Theme presets
items.description: Changing a preset updates colors, borders, shadows, radii, and other design tokens together.

items.title: An extensible base
items.description: Semantic tokens, accessible components, and responsive defaults make it easier to add your own design system.

workflow.title: Start with clear defaults instead of rebuilding the basics.
workflow.description: Decide how language, themes, and modes behave first, then put your time into the features and experience that differentiate the product.

steps.title: Choose a theme and mode
steps.description: Start with a fitting preset and set system, light, or dark as the default mode.

steps.title: Add product copy
steps.description: Maintain product copy in the matching language files as the features evolve.

steps.title: Extend the product
steps.description: Build pages and features on top of the setup while preserving consistent language and appearance preferences.

landing.footer: A reusable interface starting point for Next.js projects with language, theme, and color-mode preferences built in.

docs.title: Using NextZero
docs.introduction: NextZero brings internationalization, theme presets, and color modes together from the start. Learn how to use the included components and extend them into your own design system.

quickStart.title: Quick start
quickStart.description: After installing dependencies and starting the development server, the home page is a live environment for trying and validating every preference.

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: Follow the system or choose light and dark

switchers.title: Use the built-in switchers
switchers.description: All three components are connected to persistent state and their providers, so they can be placed directly in a header, settings page, or sidebar.
switchers.theme: Select a preset theme. It updates the theme name in Zustand and starts the root view-transition animation.
switchers.language: Change the active i18next language. The choice is remembered in browser storage.
switchers.mode: Offer system, light, and dark modes. System mode follows the operating-system preference.

customize.title: Customize the three components
customize.description: Every switcher accepts familiar button appearance props. The mode switcher also controls menu alignment and tooltip placement.

themes.title: Edit or add theme presets
themes.description: Theme presets live in one file. Their light and dark semantic tokens describe colors, shadows, radius, and fonts.
themes.tweakDescription: Adjust semantic colors in TweakCN, export the variables, and map the values to the light and dark tokens. Some included preset data was exported from or adapted from TweakCN and is listed under Apache-2.0 in THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md.

steps.1: Choose or edit a theme in TweakCN.
steps.2: Map exported colors to tokens such as primary, background, card, and border.
steps.3: Complete the tokens needed for both light and dark modes, then refresh and verify them in the theme picker.

languages.title: Edit or add languages
languages.description: Language configuration, display names, and translation files are maintained separately. Adding a language only needs configuration and one JSON file.
languages.fallback: Missing keys fall back to English, so begin by copying

docs.outro: Treat the template as your foundation: product features can evolve while language, themes, and color-mode switching stay consistent.
